text.title: Unit Testing Examples
text.summary: Worked examples: AAA pattern, parametrize, exceptions, fixtures, async, and test doubles in Python/TypeScript.

example.code:
class TestPriceCalculator: def test_calculate_total_with_tax(self): # Arrange calculator = PriceCalculator(tax_rate=Decimal("0.10")) # Act result = calculator.calculate_total(price=Decimal("100.00"), quantity=1) # Assert assert result == Decimal("110.00")
def test_calculate_total_with_zero_quantity_returns_zero(self): calculator = PriceCalculator(tax_rate=Decimal("0.10")) result = calculator.calculate_total(price=Decimal("50.00"), quantity=0) assert result == Decimal("0.00")
example.note: Blank lines between AAA phases. Each test covers one behavior.
pytest.param with id= produces readable failure messages. Named IDs beat positional indexes.
Use specific exception class, never bare pytest.raises(Exception). Assert exception attributes when they carry information.
Fixtures compose: user_service depends on sample_user. InMemoryUserRepository is a fake, not a MagicMock.
Fakes for repositories; mocks for process boundaries (email, HTTP, clock). Never mock collaborators that have testable contracts.
beforeEach recreates mocks; no shared mutable state between tests.
